title: Robust Scaling for Outliers
concept: Robust scaling uses the median and Interquartile Range (IQR) to scale features, preventing extreme outliers from distorting the scale.

exercises.prompt: Warmup: Why does a single extreme outlier hurt Min-Max scaling more than standardization?

options.text: It forces all the other normal data points to get compressed into a very narrow range near 0 or 1
It changes the categorical values into text
It forces the standard deviation to be exactly zero

exercises.explain: Yes! Min-Max uses the absolute maximum, so a massive outlier will pull the max way up and squash the rest of the data.

cards.title: A room with a billionaire
cards.body: If 9 teachers making $50k sit in a room with a billionaire, their average income is $100 million. But the middle value (median) is still $50k. Averages can misrepresent a group when outliers exist.

cards.title: Robust Scaling
cards.body: Robust scaling scales features using statistics immune to outliers. It subtracts the median (the middle value) and divides by the Interquartile Range (IQR) (the range of the middle 50%).

cards.title: Diving judges
cards.body: In gymnastics or diving, judges throw out the highest and lowest scores to get a 'robust' measure of performance. Robust scaling does this mathematically by ignoring the top and bottom 25%.

cards.title: Developer Salaries
cards.body: In a salary dataset ranging from $50k to $150k with a few executives making $5,000,000, robust scaling uses the median salary ($90k) and IQR ($40k). This keeps normal salaries spread out and distinct.

cards.title: Z-score has limits
cards.body: Why not just use Z-score standardization? Z-score uses mean and standard deviation. A single outlier drags the mean up and inflates the standard deviation, distorting the scores of everyone else!

cards.title: Handling Outliers
cards.body: How Standard vs. Robust Scaling respond to outliers:

left.text:
Uses mean & std dev.
Outliers pull the mean.
Normal values cluster tightly.

right.text:
Uses median & IQR.
Outliers are ignored.
Normal values stay spread out.

exercises.prompt: Which two statistical metrics does Robust Scaling use to transform features?
exercises.explain: Robust Scaling centers data around the median and scales it using the IQR (middle 50% range) to remain outlier-resistant.

exercises.prompt: True or false: The Interquartile Range (IQR) is heavily influenced by extreme outliers.
exercises.explain: False! The IQR is the range of the middle 50% of the data, so it completely ignores the top 25% and bottom 25% where outliers live.

exercises.sentence: Robust scaling subtracts the ___ and divides by the Interquartile Range.
exercises.explain: Subtracting the median centers the feature at its midpoint, which is robust against skewness from outliers.

exercises.prompt: Which scaler should you choose for a feature with extreme, unpredictable outliers that you cannot remove?
exercises.explain: Robust Scaler uses median and IQR, making it the only scaler here that is not distorted by extreme outliers.

exercises.prompt: You are preparing a dataset of home sizes in a city. Most houses are 1,000 to 3,000 sq ft, but there are a few massive castles over 100,000 sq ft. If you use a standard scaler, the castles will inflate the standard deviation, making all normal houses look almost identical to the model. How should you scale this feature?

options.text: Use Robust Scaler, which centers on the median and scales by the IQR, keeping the normal house sizes distinct.
Use Min-Max scaling to force everything between 0 and 1.
Avoid scaling entirely and use the raw square footage.

exercises.explain: Correct! Robust Scaler ignores the extreme castle sizes when defining the scale of the middle 50% of homes. This keeps normal home differences highly visible to the model. Sensational work completing Unit 8 on Feature Scaling & Normalization!
